explicit_text: I grew up in Redondo Beach, California, where life on the beach was vibrant and full of energy during my youth. The amusement zone was a central part of the community, surrounded by rumrunners and beach-front gangs, which deeply influenced my artistic vision. This unique environment shaped much of my early work and memories.

explicit_text: Los Angeles was central to my life and career, especially during the 1950s and 1960s art scene. The city had a dynamic but challenging art environment, with an influential art 'syndicate' and a divide between Funk art and the 'L.A. Look.' I worked with local galleries and knew artists like Edward Kienholz and Wallace Berman, which shaped my experience.
